claimText: The Igla-S is a Russian-origin man-portable, shoulder-launched surface-to-air missile (SAM) system.
evidenceExcerpt: Indian Army received new Russian Igla-S missiles to counter drones, choppers, jets. About Igla-S: It is a man-portable, shoulder-launched surface-to-air missile (SAM) system
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and type of the Igla-S missile system.

claimText: The Igla-S missile system utilizes infrared (IR) homing to detect and lock onto the heat signatures of aerial targets.
evidenceExcerpt: It uses infrared (IR) homing to detect and lock onto the heat signatures of aerial targets.
examUse: Statement-based question on the guidance mechanism of the Igla-S.

claimText: The Igla-S missile system has an engagement range of up to 6 km and can reach altitudes up to 3.5 km.
evidenceExcerpt: It can engage targets at ranges up to 6 km and altitudes up to 3.5 km.
examUse: Fact-check statement in a multi-statement question regarding the operational range of Igla-S.

claimText: The Igla-S is classified as an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS).
evidenceExcerpt: It is an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS).
examUse: Classification or correct-pair question matching Igla-S to VSHORADS.
